title: The Latest Time to Catch a Bus
description:
You are given a 0-indexed integer array buses of length n, where buses[i] represents the departure time of the ith bus. You are also given a 0-indexed integer array passengers of length m, where passengers[j] represents the arrival time of the jth passenger. All bus departure times are unique. All passenger arrival times are unique.

You are given an integer capacity, which represents the maximum number of passengers that can get on each bus.

When a passenger arrives, they will wait in line for the next available bus. You can get on a bus that departs at x minutes if you arrive at y minutes where y <= x, and the bus is not full. Passengers with the earliest arrival times get on the bus first.

More formally when a bus arrives, either:

*   If capacity or fewer passengers are waiting for a bus, they will all get on the bus, or
*   The capacity passengers with the earliest arrival times will get on the bus.

Return _the latest time you may arrive at the bus station to catch a bus_. You cannot arrive at the same time as another passenger.

Note: The arrays buses and passengers are not necessarily sorted.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def last_passenger_time(buses, passengers, capacity):
    buses.sort()
    passengers.sort()

    last_index = 0
    for bus in buses:
        cnt = 0
        while last_index < len(passengers) and passengers[last_index] <= bus and cnt < capacity:
            cnt += 1
            last_index += 1

    return buses[0] - 1 if last_index == 0 else passengers[last_index - 1] - 1
```
    
    1. Sort both `buses` and `passengers` arrays in increasing order.
2. Initialize the variable `lastIndex` to 0, which will keep track of the index of the last passenger that got on a bus.
3. Iterate through the sorted `buses` array.
4. For each bus, count how many passengers can get on it (up to `capacity`). Update the `lastIndex` as passengers get on the bus.
5. After iterating through all buses, if `lastIndex` is still 0, return the first bus departure time minus 1. Otherwise, return the arrival time of the last passenger who got on a bus minus 1. This is because you cannot arrive at the same time as another passenger.
